text:
### 2. Risk & Compliance (Head: Chief Risk Officer)  
**Mission:** Protect FirstUK Bank through proactive risk identification and regulatory compliance  
**Key Functions:**
- Compliance (see [[departments]])
- Financial Crime (see [[departments]])
- Fraud Prevention
- Risk Assessment
- Internal Audit  
**Regulatory Responsibilities:**
- AML/KYC compliance (per [[business_ontology]])
- Customer Due Diligence (CDD)
- Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) for high-risk customers
- Suspicious Activity Reporting (SAR)  
**Key Policies:**
- Customer Identification Policy (CID-POL-001)
- AML Policy (AML-POL-002)
- Operational Risk Policy (ORP-POL-003)
- Privacy Policy (PRI-POL-004)  
---
name: Chunk 8/24

raw_entities.name: Risk & Compliance
raw_entities.description: A core internal department responsible for proactive risk identification and regulatory compliance. It oversees key functional areas including compliance, financial crime, fraud prevention, risk assessment, and internal audit to protect the bank's operational integrity.
context: Mission: Protect FirstUK Bank through proactive risk identification and regulatory compliance

raw_entities.name: Chief Risk Officer
raw_entities.description: The executive leadership role serving as the head of the Risk & Compliance department. This position holds defined accountability for overseeing proactive risk identification and ensuring regulatory compliance across FirstUK Bank.

raw_entities.name: Compliance
raw_entities.description: A specialized functional area within the Risk & Compliance department focused on ensuring the bank adheres to external regulations and internal governance standards. It operates as a key oversight function alongside financial crime and audit.

raw_entities.name: Financial Crime
raw_entities.description: A dedicated functional area responsible for preventing, detecting, and reporting financial crimes. It operates as a key component of the Risk & Compliance department's operational structure.

raw_entities.name: Internal Audit
raw_entities.description: A key functional area listed under Risk & Compliance responsible for independent evaluation of the bank's internal controls, risk management frameworks, and governance processes.

raw_entities.name: AML/KYC Compliance
aliases: Anti-Money Laundering / Know Your Customer Compliance
raw_entities.description: A core regulatory compliance concept encompassing anti-money laundering and know your customer procedures. It is explicitly mandated as a primary regulatory responsibility for the Risk & Compliance department, governed by the bank's business ontology.

raw_entities.name: Customer Due Diligence
raw_entities.description: A mandatory KYC verification process applied to customers as part of the bank's regulatory responsibilities. It serves as the baseline procedure for assessing customer risk profiles before onboarding.

raw_entities.name: Enhanced Due Diligence
raw_entities.description: A heightened KYC verification procedure applied specifically to high-risk customers. It requires deeper scrutiny, ongoing monitoring, and additional documentation beyond standard due diligence requirements.
context: Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) for high-risk customers

raw_entities.name: Suspicious Activity Reporting
raw_entities.description: A regulatory reporting obligation requiring the bank to formally document and submit reports regarding identified suspicious financial activities or transactions. It is a key compliance output managed by the Risk & Compliance function.

raw_entities.name: Fraud Prevention
raw_entities.description: A core operational concept focused on identifying, mitigating, and preventing fraudulent activities across the bank's products and services. It is listed as a key function under the Risk & Compliance department.

raw_entities.name: Risk Assessment
raw_entities.description: A systematic process for identifying, analyzing, and evaluating potential risks to the bank's operations, capital, and reputation. It is designated as a key function within the Risk & Compliance department's operational scope.

raw_entities.name: Customer Identification Policy
raw_entities.description: A formal governance policy establishing the mandatory procedures and standards for verifying customer identities. It is designated with internal policy identifier CID-POL-001 and serves as a foundational compliance requirement.

raw_entities.name: AML Policy
raw_entities.description: A core compliance policy outlining the bank's framework and controls for preventing money laundering. It is designated with internal policy identifier AML-POL-002 and enforces regulatory obligations across financial operations.

raw_entities.name: Operational Risk Policy
raw_entities.description: A governance policy defining the bank's approach to identifying, assessing, and mitigating operational risks. It is designated with internal policy identifier ORP-POL-003 and establishes mandatory control standards.

raw_entities.name: Privacy Policy
raw_entities.description: A compliance policy governing the handling, protection, and processing of customer data in accordance with privacy regulations. It is designated with internal policy identifier PRI-POL-004 and enforces data protection obligations.

properties.regulatory_significance: Core regulatory responsibility mandated for the Risk & Compliance unit
Mandatory compliance requirement for standard customer verification

properties.definition: Intensive due diligence process applied to high-risk customers

properties.regulatory_significance: Mandatory reporting obligation to regulatory authorities

relationships.description: The Chief Risk Officer leads the Compliance unit within the Risk & Compliance department.
relationships.context: Risk & Compliance (Head: Chief Risk Officer) ... Key Functions: Compliance

relationships.description: The Chief Risk Officer leads the Financial Crime unit responsible for regulatory compliance and fraud monitoring.
relationships.context: Risk & Compliance (Head: Chief Risk Officer) ... Key Functions: Financial Crime

relationships.description: The Chief Risk Officer leads the Internal Audit function to ensure independent oversight of risk controls.
relationships.context: Risk & Compliance (Head: Chief Risk Officer) ... Key Functions: Internal Audit

relationships.description: The Compliance unit manages Customer Due Diligence processes to verify customer identities and assess baseline risk.
relationships.context: Key Functions: Compliance ... Regulatory Responsibilities: Customer Due Diligence (CDD)

relationships.description: The Financial Crime unit is directly responsible for end-to-end AML/KYC compliance across the bank.
The Financial Crime unit manages Enhanced Due Diligence procedures specifically for high-risk customer segments.
relationships.context: Regulatory Responsibilities: Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) for high-risk customers

relationships.description: The Financial Crime unit performs Suspicious Activity Reporting to regulatory bodies upon detecting red flags.
Internal Audit independently reviews Risk Assessment methodologies to ensure accuracy and regulatory alignment.
relationships.context: Key Functions: Risk Assessment ... Internal Audit

relationships.description: The Chief Risk Officer oversees Fraud Prevention strategies as a core component of the bank's risk mitigation framework.
The Compliance unit enforces the Customer Identification Policy to ensure all onboarding meets KYC standards.
The Financial Crime unit enforces the AML Policy across all customer lifecycle touchpoints.
Internal Audit enforces the Operational Risk Policy by conducting independent control testing and gap analysis.
The Chief Risk Officer owns the Privacy Policy, ensuring data handling aligns with regulatory and customer trust standards.
AML/KYC Compliance activities are strictly governed by the bank's overarching AML Policy framework.
relationships.context: Regulatory Responsibilities: AML/KYC compliance ... Key Policies: AML Policy (AML-POL-002)

relationships.description: Customer Due Diligence procedures are governed by the Customer Identification Policy to standardize verification steps.
relationships.context: Regulatory Responsibilities: Customer Due Diligence (CDD) ... Key Policies: Customer Identification Policy (CID-POL-001)

relationships.description: Enhanced Due Diligence protocols are governed by the AML Policy to address elevated risk profiles.
relationships.context: Regulatory Responsibilities: Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) ... Key Policies: AML Policy (AML-POL-002)

relationships.description: Suspicious Activity Reporting workflows are governed by the AML Policy to ensure timely and accurate regulatory disclosures.
relationships.context: Regulatory Responsibilities: Suspicious Activity Reporting (SAR) ... Key Policies: AML Policy (AML-POL-002)
